days.code: Launch Day — public identity + repo scaffold. (1) Rewrite LinkedIn headline to Staff Engineer | Distributed Systems + AI Infra | Rust · Kafka · Kubernetes | ex-Agoda 1.5T/day TSDB · Delivery Hero · Walmart 7M sensors; refresh About with strongest numbers. (2) Rewrite GitHub profile README: who you are (2 lines), what you're building (flagship project), technical bets (Rust infra + AI observability), 'Currently building' placeholder. Dark-mode friendly, no emojis. (3) Create repo infra-ai-streaming: README only today — problem statement (why existing observability fails for LLM inference at scale), Mermaid architecture overview, target metrics (1M events/min, sub-100ms ingestion P99). Pin repo to profile.

experience.title: 1.5 Trillion Events Per Day — Why We Couldn't Buy Observability
experience.subtitle: Agoda · WhiteFalcon TSDB · the day public building started
experience.bridge: Today I opened infra-ai-streaming because the same cardinality and throughput constraints I lived with at Agoda are now hitting every team shipping LLM inference.

ai.title: Day 0 — The Inference Problem Is a Data Plane Problem
ai.subtitle: Why LLM observability belongs next to your TSDB, not your APM sidebar
ai.hook: Before KV cache math: inference telemetry is just another high-cardinality stream — and you already know how those fail.

days.thread: Agoda-scale ingestion pain is the reason today's README defines model_id × tenant_id as first-class partition keys.

days.code: DESIGN.md for infra-ai-streaming (Staff-level artifact before more code): (1) Problem & Goals, (2) CAP — why AP over CP for ingestion, (3) Partition strategy — avoid hot partitions on model_id, (4) Backpressure — channel-based Rust ingestion, (5) Failure modes — Kafka down, ClickHouse slow, (6) Horizontal scaling plan, (7) Consistency model for analytics queries. This doc drives every later schema field.

experience.title: Design Docs Beat Code on Day One
experience.subtitle: Staff interviews read DESIGN.md before they read main.rs
experience.bridge: The CAP, partition, and backpressure sections I wrote today are the contract the Rust ingestion code must obey tomorrow.

ai.title: Day 1 — Transformer Inference Mechanics for Infra Engineers
ai.subtitle: Prefill vs decode, KV cache, and why latency has two phases
ai.hook: Karpathy's 'build GPT' at 1.5× — skip the training; watch the autoregressive loop and memory growth per token.

days.thread: DESIGN.md's event schema gets prefill_ms and decode_ms fields because Blog B's two-phase latency model maps directly to observability SLOs.

days.code: Ticket G-01 — Rust ingestion core: Axum HTTP server with POST /ingest accepting batched JSON events (model_id, latency_ms, prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, cost_usd, tenant_id). Async Kafka producer via rdkafka. Channel-based backpressure with configurable buffer size. Graceful shutdown. Verify events reach Kafka topic locally. Commit messages describe design decisions, not 'added ingestion'.

experience.title: Ceph, POSIX, and the Lie of 'Just Use a Filesystem'
experience.subtitle: Agoda · storage layer · zero-copy writes in Rust
experience.bridge: Building /ingest with channel backpressure today mirrors how we avoided blocking the hot path when Ceph writes couldn't keep up with ingest bursts.

ai.title: Day 2 — Continuous Batching in vLLM
ai.subtitle: Why GPU utilization is a queueing problem, not a FLOPs problem
ai.hook: Draw static vs continuous batching as Kafka consumer poll loops — same insight, different silicon.

days.thread: Kafka batching in G-01 and vLLM continuous batching share one design rule: never wait for a full batch if the queue is starving.

days.code: Ticket G-01 — Rust ingestion core: Axum POST /ingest for batched JSON events (model_id, latency_ms, prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, cost_usd, tenant_id). rdkafka async producer, channel backpressure, graceful shutdown. Verify events on Kafka topic. Publish Experience blog (Agoda quantile merge) + AI Learning Day 2 (continuous batching) if not already live.

experience.title: When Percentiles Lie — Cross-Tier P95/P99 in a 1.8T/Day TSDB
experience.subtitle: Agoda · histogram merge across Redis hot + S3/Parquet cold
experience.bridge: LensAI dashboards cannot average P99 across tenants — same merge discipline as WhiteFalcon quantile queries.

ai.title: Day 2 of Learning LLM Inference — Continuous Batching in vLLM
ai.subtitle: GPU scheduler design as a queueing problem
ai.hook: Ingestion batching in Rust mirrors continuous batching — never starve the queue waiting for a full batch.

days.thread: Rust /ingest backpressure today implements the same 'keep the worker busy' rule as vLLM continuous batching.

days.code: Ticket G-02 — docker-compose.yml: Redpanda, ClickHouse, Redis, Grafana, Prometheus (`docker compose up`). Go consumer reads Kafka, logs stdout (ClickHouse writer ships Day 5). Wire Rust → Kafka → Go end-to-end with test event. Publish both blogs; share URLs only.

experience.title: Seven Million IoT Sensors — Failure Modes Textbooks Skip
experience.subtitle: Walmart · device identity · edge filtering at scale
experience.bridge: Multi-service compose today mirrors shard isolation before you touch million-device fan-out.

ai.title: Day 3 of Learning LLM Inference — Token Budgets and Real Cost Structure
ai.subtitle: Prompt vs completion pricing as capacity planning
ai.hook: Validate cost_usd + prompt vs completion token fields in event JSON against provider pricing pages.

days.thread: Go consumer skeleton exists so tomorrow's ClickHouse writer can aggregate cost_usd the way finance asks.
days.title: Local dev stack + Go consumer skeleton

days.code: Ticket G-03 — ClickHouse batch writer: 1000 events or 500ms flush; circuit breaker; Redis overflow; DLQ after 3 retries. OBSERVABILITY.md — self-metrics for the pipeline (P50/P95/P99, lag, DLQ depth).

experience.title: Five Thousand Geo-Events Per Second — Shape of the Stream
experience.subtitle: Delivery Hero · OSRM · throughput as a schema decision
experience.bridge: OTel spans on the ingestion path today tag the same dimensions I'd use to debug a map-matching pipeline under peak dinner rush.

ai.title: Day 5 — Tensor Parallelism Meets Kafka Partitions
ai.subtitle: Model serving scale-out as a sharding problem
ai.hook: Triton's model repository is just a partition assignment table with GPUs instead of brokers.

days.thread: BENCHMARKS.md numbers are meaningless without labels — today's metrics mirror how I'd prove DH routing didn't drop events under load.

days.code: Ticket G-05 — Grafana dashboard (4 panels): (1) ingestion throughput events/sec by tenant, (2) P99 inference latency by model_id, (3) token cost/hour per tenant, (4) Kafka consumer lag trend. Export JSON to /dashboards; screenshot for README. Update resume Open Source section + LinkedIn Featured with infra-ai-streaming link.

experience.title: Cardinality Is the Silent Killer — RoaringBitmap Lessons
experience.subtitle: Agoda · Prometheus walls · model_id × tenant_id
experience.bridge: The Grafana P99-by-model_id panel only works if I refused to explode label cardinality the way we learned at Agoda scale.

ai.title: Day 6 — Sampling and Deterministic Routing
ai.subtitle: Why 1% trace sampling isn't free randomness
ai.hook: Consistent hashing for routes and consistent sampling for traces — same hash function, different failure modes.

days.thread: Four Grafana panels are the user-facing proof that Blog A's cardinality war and Blog B's sampling discipline share one schema.

days.code: Ticket G-06 — Token-bucket rate limiting in Rust ingestion per tenant_id (Redis shared counter for multi-instance). Per-tenant config: max events/sec, burst. Return 429 + Retry-After when exceeded. Write CHAOS.md: 5 failure scenarios — Kafka broker dies mid-ingest, ClickHouse timeout, Redis lost, ingestion OOM, network partition — expected behavior and recovery for each.

experience.title: Supplier APIs and Token Buckets — Wayfair's Real Circuit Breaker
experience.subtitle: Rate limits that survived 10× surprise load
experience.bridge: Redis token buckets on tenant_id today are the pricing-API pattern I shipped — 429 with Retry-After, not silent drop.

ai.title: Day 7 — Quantization vs Compression Tradeoffs
ai.subtitle: INT8/INT4 as Snappy-vs-Zstd for weights
ai.hook: You already pick codecs by access pattern — quantization is picking precision by deployment budget.

days.thread: CHAOS.md scenario 3 (Redis lost) exists because rate limiting without a fallback store is a decorated denial-of-service.

days.code: Ticket G-07 — Helm charts for full stack: ingestion-engine HPA on custom Kafka lag metric, Redpanda, ClickHouse, Redis. Resource requests/limits, liveness/readiness probes, PodDisruptionBudget maxUnavailable:1, ConfigMap for tenant config. Deploy to local k3d; verify end-to-end. Add deploy/README.md with 3-command deploy.

experience.title: Ten Thousand Concurrent Requests — EKS Patterns That Actually Helped
experience.subtitle: Delivery Hero · peak · HPA limits
experience.bridge: Helm + HPA on Kafka lag is how I'd run DH peak today — scale on consumer lag, not CPU theater.

ai.title: Day 8 — Prompt Caching at the Infrastructure Layer
ai.subtitle: Prefix reuse as KV-cache hit rate for dollars
ai.hook: Anthropic/OpenAI prompt cache headers are cache-control for transformers — treat them like HTTP CDN rules.

days.thread: HPA custom metric wiring is Blog A's peak-load story applied to the ingestion Deployment Blog B's cache economics justify.

days.code: Portfolio site v1 on GitHub Pages: name, positioning, 3 project cards with status badges, LinkedIn/GitHub links, Writing section. Minimal dark HTML, no frameworks. URL goes on resume header.

experience.title: 250k SKUs in Sub-Second — Event-Driven vs Polling
experience.subtitle: Wayfair · price propagation · why streams win
experience.bridge: Publishing the portfolio site today is shipping a read model — same as making pricing updates visible without batch lag.

ai.title: Day 9 — RAG as an Infra Pipeline
ai.subtitle: Chunk → embed → index → retrieve → generate latency budget
ai.hook: RAG's bottleneck is never the LLM first — it's the fan-in on your vector store's QPS.

days.thread: Site 'Writing' section links will point at posts that explain pipelines — today's code is another pipeline, different payload.

days.code: chaos/run_chaos.sh: (1) kill Redpanda mid-ingest, restart after 30s — verify zero loss via DLQ + WAL replay, (2) throttle ClickHouse — verify circuit breaker + Redis buffer, (3) run at 10k events/sec, capture metrics. Update CHAOS.md with actual results + Grafana screenshots.

project_blog.title: Week 2 — Production Hardening: Chaos, Rate Limits, and Helm
project_blog.subtitle: Why README screenshots matter more than feature lists

experience.title: We Killed Redpanda on Purpose — Chaos as Commit Message
experience.subtitle: Agoda · Kafka · proving recovery beats claiming it
experience.bridge: run_chaos.sh results go into CHAOS.md the way we'd attach postmortems to JIRA — evidence, not vibes.

ai.title: Day 10 — GPU Memory Management for Non-CUDA Engineers
ai.subtitle: VRAM, weight loading, and batch-size cliffs
ai.hook: OOM on a GPU is OOM on a JVM — someone's batch size is a lie.

days.thread: Chaos test 2 (slow ClickHouse) validates the circuit breaker Blog A lived through in tiered storage and Blog B's memory-bound batching story predicts.

days.code: OSS-01 — First contribution to VictoriaMetrics or Vector.dev (good-first-issue / help-wanted). Documentation example, benchmark, or reproducible bug report counts. Submit PR; link in weekly notes.

experience.title: Reading VictoriaMetrics Source at 11pm — OSS as Interview Prep
experience.subtitle: What production Rust teaches that tutorials skip
experience.bridge: Today's OSS PR is practice for reading Agoda-scale codebases under time pressure — the skill transfers directly.

ai.title: Day 11 — Serving Frameworks Compared as Queue Schedulers
ai.subtitle: vLLM vs TGI vs Ollama — scheduling policies, not logos
ai.hook: Ask 'what's the queue model?' before 'what's the benchmark score?'

days.thread: Anomaly detection on model_id latency is only trustworthy if you understand how each serving framework reports time (OSS reading informs that).

days.code: Ticket G-09 — Anomaly detection in Go consumer: z-score on inference latency per model_id (sliding window 100 points, flag if >3σ). On anomaly: Kafka topic anomalies, Prometheus anomalies_detected_total, Grafana alert rule.

experience.title: OTA at Scale — At-Least-Once Is a Feature, Not a Bug
experience.subtitle: Walmart · firmware · Kafka offsets in disguise
experience.bridge: z-score anomaly detection is edge filtering moved upstream — catch the bad device before it poisons aggregates.

ai.title: Day 12 — Semantic Caching vs Exact-Match Redis
ai.subtitle: Embeddings as cache keys — false positive risk as SLA
ai.hook: Semantic cache hit = approximate nearest neighbor with business consequences — tune threshold like tail latency.

days.thread: The anomalies topic is where exact rules end — semantic cache false positives will land in the same operational queue.

days.code: Week 2 README polish for infra-ai-streaming: Excalidraw architecture PNG, Design Decisions (3 tradeoffs), Benchmarks table, Chaos Testing summary, Getting Started under 5 commands. README should stand alone for hiring committees.

experience.title: Two Weeks, One README — Hiring Committees Scroll
experience.subtitle: Proof beats promises · screenshots > adjectives
experience.bridge: README polish today is what I'd send a Staff panel instead of a slide deck — architecture, benchmarks, chaos, quickstart.

ai.title: Day 13 — Embeddings as Dense Time-Series IDs
ai.subtitle: Vectors are just high-dimensional series with ANN indexes
ai.hook: HNSW under write load feels like SSTable compaction under read pressure — you've already ops'd this class of problem.

days.thread: Week-2 README closes infra-ai-streaming; tomorrow ebpf-llm-tracer extends observability to apps that will never install your SDK.

days.code: Create repo ebpf-llm-tracer + DESIGN.md: zero-SDK LLM HTTP tracing via eBPF. Sections: probe attachment points (socket/connect/send), HTTP parsing for model_id and token headers, userspace agent architecture, security/CAP constraints, integration contract with infra-ai-streaming Kafka topic. Read Cilium + BCC docs; spike feasibility on local Linux VM or Docker privileged container.

experience.title: High-Cardinality Metrics — The Prometheus Wall We Hit
experience.subtitle: Agoda · schema design · label explosions
experience.bridge: ebpf-llm-tracer DESIGN.md caps label cardinality because I watched model_id × pod × zone destroy a metrics stack once.

ai.title: Day 14 — eBPF for AI Infrastructure
ai.subtitle: Kernel probes vs SDK patches — when zero-instrumentation wins
ai.hook: Cilium proved network observability without app changes — LLM HTTP is the next syscall surface.

days.thread: Zero-SDK tracing is the design decision: Blog A's cardinality discipline meets Blog B's probe attachment strategy in one Kafka topic.

days.code: eBPF program v0 — trace socket syscalls for outbound HTTPS to api.openai.com / api.anthropic.com. BPF maps for connection → PID correlation. Userspace loader in Rust or Go using libbpf-rs / cilium/ebpf. Log raw connect events; no parsing yet.

experience.title: Async Pipelines That Survived Dinner Rush
experience.subtitle: Delivery Hero · SQS/Kinesis · decoupling notifications from analytics
experience.bridge: Socket-level probes today are the lightest-weight tap on a path that must not add milliseconds DH couldn't afford.

ai.title: Day 15 — Multi-Model Routing Strategies
ai.subtitle: Cost-first vs quality-first vs latency-first — pick two
ai.hook: Routing is policy engines — you operated feature flags for humans; models are flags with GPUs.

days.thread: Syscall capture v0 is useless without a routing policy later — flagd will decide which model the probe's latency belongs to.

days.code: HTTP parsing in userspace: reassemble TLS plaintext via uprobes or capture pre-TLS on localhost test server first. Extract method, path, status, Content-Length, x-request-id, model from JSON body snippets. Unit tests with canned PCAP fixtures.

experience.title: Cross-Tier Query Latency — Hot Redis, Cold Ceph
experience.subtitle: Agoda · tiering · why recent data dominates SLOs
experience.bridge: HTTP parsing in userspace mirrors separating hot path parsing from cold storage — do the minimum work before the bus.

ai.title: Day 16 — The Mental Model That Made LLM Infra Click
ai.subtitle: Prefill/decode + KV cache + continuous batching in DS analogies
ai.hook: If you can explain Kafka, you can explain inference — after you name the two phases.

days.thread: Parsed model_id fields in eBPF events must align with the two-phase latency columns Blog B introduced on Day 1.

days.code: Map parsed HTTP events to infra-ai-streaming ingest schema (model_id, latency_ms, prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, cost_usd, tenant_id, source=ebpf). Kafka producer from ebpf-llm-tracer userspace agent. End-to-end: curl OpenAI-compatible mock → eBPF → Kafka → existing consumer.

project_blog.title: Week 3 — eBPF Zero-SDK Tracing for LLM HTTP
project_blog.subtitle: ebpf-llm-tracer architecture and Kafka integration

experience.title: Building in Public — The Blog Post That Outlives LinkedIn
experience.subtitle: Architecture posts as durable hiring signals
experience.bridge: Kafka wiring from ebpf-llm-tracer reuses the ingestion contract I benchmarked — platform thinking is reuse, not rewrite.

ai.title: Day 17 — LLM Observability — What Actually Matters
ai.subtitle: Arize vs Langfuse vs Helicone — schema diff against your events
ai.hook: Add fields for tool spans and cache hits now — retrofitting schema is Agoda cardinality pain again.

days.thread: End-to-end eBPF → ClickHouse proves LensAI's thesis: inference telemetry without vendor SDK lock-in.

days.code: Ticket G-10 pattern on ingestion path — add WAL to Rust ingestion (if not merged): append-only segments before Kafka produce, replay on startup for un-acked segments. Metrics wal_segments_pending, wal_replay_events_total. Parallel: harden ebpf-llm-tracer with rate limiting and drop policy when Kafka backpressures.

experience.title: Rate Limiting at the Supplier Boundary — Not the Textbook Diagram
experience.subtitle: Wayfair · token bucket in production
experience.bridge: WAL on ingestion plus ebpf backpressure policy is the same 'never lose the write' instinct as supplier API durability.

ai.title: Day 18 — Quantization and Model Optimization
ai.subtitle: GPTQ vs AWQ vs bitsandbytes — pick variants like codec picks
ai.hook: Quantized weights are a deployment flag — infra owns the matrix of {cost, latency, quality}.

days.thread: wal_replay_events_total and probe drop counters belong in the same Grafana board — both are durability under overload.

days.code: ebpf-llm-tracer demo harness: docker-compose sidecar deployment, capture live calls to mock LLM server with zero app SDK changes. BENCHMARKS.md for probe overhead (CPU %, P99 latency delta). Document required kernel ≥5.10 and CAP_BPF.

experience.title: Kafka + Redis Tiering — Query Latency by Temperature
experience.subtitle: Agoda · decoupling hot reads from cold storage
experience.bridge: Demo harness with mock LLM proves the tracer without production keys — same staging discipline as Agoda load tests.

ai.title: Day 19 — Agent Infrastructure — Tools, Memory, Loops
ai.subtitle: Production agents need queues, idempotency, and traces
ai.hook: Tool calling is RPC with hallucination risk — your streaming stack already handles worse RPC fan-out.

days.thread: ebpf overhead benchmarks set the budget TraceForge will need when agents make ten HTTP calls per user request.

days.code: OSS-02 — Kafka or Redpanda issue/PR in consumer group rebalancing or partition assignment. ebpf-llm-tracer README: architecture diagram, demo GIF, comparison table vs SDK instrumentation (Overhead, coverage, security).

experience.title: Peak Kubernetes — HPA Reacts, It Doesn't Predict
experience.subtitle: Delivery Hero · 10k concurrent · PDBs during deploys
experience.bridge: Second OSS PR on Kafka rebalancing is credibility for the bus every LensAI component shares.

ai.title: Day 20 — Prompt Engineering as Infra Optimization
ai.subtitle: Prompt cache and prefix reuse in dollars per million requests
ai.hook: Calculate savings at 1M req/day — that's the slide your PM actually reads.

days.thread: Tracer README comparison table is the buyer's guide; prompt cache math is the CFO's guide — same product.

days.code: Create distributed-flagd + DESIGN.md: (1) Problem — commercial flag SaaS cost at scale, (2) etcd consensus + watch propagation, (3) gRPC streaming to edge clients, (4) AI model rollout flags — percentage traffic between model versions, (5) audit log design.

experience.title: LaunchDarkly Money — Why We Build flagd Ourselves
experience.subtitle: Platform economics · control plane vs data plane
experience.bridge: etcd + gRPC streaming today is DH real-time config with a different payload — model version instead of surge multiplier.

ai.title: Day 21 — Production Reliability for LLM APIs
ai.subtitle: Rate limits, provider outages, streaming disconnects — mapped to patterns you know
ai.hook: Map every provider error code to: retry, circuit break, fallback model, or fail loud.

days.thread: Percentage rollout flags are how Blog B's routing strategies become Blog A's change-management story ops teams trust.

days.code: distributed-flagd core in Go: HTTP CRUD for flags, etcd backend with watch, gRPC streaming push to clients. Flag types: boolean, string, percentage rollout. Docker Compose with etcd single-node for local dev.

experience.title: H3 vs Bounding Boxes — Geospatial Indexing That Scales
experience.subtitle: Delivery Hero · surge detection · why naive geo fails
experience.bridge: Sticky user hashing for model flags copies H3 cell assignment — same user, same variant, all day.

ai.title: Day 22 — Feature Flags for Model Rollouts
ai.subtitle: Canary models with audit trails
ai.hook: A model rollout without audit log is a production incident waiting for a postmortem.

days.thread: resolved_model_id on ingest events closes the loop between flagd policy and ClickHouse cost attribution.

days.code: Model rollout evaluator: given tenant_id + user_id hash, resolve active model version from percentage flag. Integration hook: infra-ai-streaming ingestion adds resolved_model_id label from flagd sidecar. Tests for sticky assignment and 50/50 split stability.

experience.title: OSRM at 5000 Events/sec — When ETA Becomes Infrastructure
experience.subtitle: Delivery Hero · route recompute budget
experience.bridge: CRD + Helm for flags is how platform teams let app engineers self-serve rollouts without SSH.

ai.title: Day 23 — Evaluations as Event Streams
ai.subtitle: Eval harnesses are Kafka topics with scores
ai.hook: LLM-as-judge output is just another event type — store it like you store metrics.

days.thread: Live demo flipping traffic between models is the TraceForge preview — multi-step workflows need the same visibility.

days.code: Kubernetes CRD for FlagDefinition + Helm chart. HPA not required; document upgrade strategy for flag schema changes. Demo: flip 10% → 50% → 100% traffic to gpt-4o-mini vs gpt-4o with Grafana cost panel showing split.

project_blog.title: Week 4 — distributed-flagd and Model Rollouts
project_blog.subtitle: etcd, gRPC streaming, and percentage canaries

experience.title: BigQuery Streaming vs Batch — Burst Traffic Truth
experience.subtitle: Wayfair · data platform · slots and surprises
experience.bridge: Audit Kafka topic for flags is BigQuery streaming inserts done right — append-only, replayable, owned.

ai.title: Day 24 — GPU Scheduling as Resource Management
ai.subtitle: MIG, tensor parallelism, Kubernetes extended resources
ai.hook: You don't need CUDA — you need a scheduler that knows VRAM is finite.

days.thread: etcd leader-loss chaos test is the flag analog of a BigQuery slot exhaustion — degrade gracefully, not randomly.

days.code: Audit log: append-only Kafka topic flag_audit with who changed what flag when. CLI traceforge-flagctl for emergency kill-switch on model routes. CHAOS test: etcd leader loss during rollout — clients must fail safe to last-known-good flags.

experience.title: Distributed Redis Rate Limits — Lua Scripts and Race Conditions
experience.subtitle: Wayfair · sliding window in production
experience.bridge: flagctl kill-switch is the pricing freeze switch we wished we'd had one Black Friday.

ai.title: Day 25 — Cost Models for LLM Gateways
ai.subtitle: Cache hit rate × cheaper model route × prompt cache
ai.hook: Build the spreadsheet before the Rust — numbers justify the repo.

days.thread: BENCHMARKS on flag evaluation QPS proves control-plane latency won't starve data-plane ingestion.

days.code: distributed-flagd BENCHMARKS.md: flag evaluation QPS, watch propagation latency P99, etcd write throughput. Cross-link ebpf + streaming repos in each README 'Platform' section.

experience.title: Systems That Outlast Their Architects — Walmart Lessons
experience.subtitle: Documentation, simplicity, operability
experience.bridge: OpenAPI + SDK stub is writing for the team that inherits LensAI — Walmart taught me they'll exist.

ai.title: Day 26 — Fine-Tuning vs RAG vs Prompting — Infra Cost View
ai.subtitle: When to buy GPUs vs buy vectors vs buy nothing
ai.hook: Staff engineers pick the cheapest path that meets SLO — not the coolest paper.

days.thread: Three-repo cross-links in READMEs are the platform story recruiters ask for in month five — start showing it now.

days.code: distributed-flagd polish: OpenAPI spec, client SDK stub (Go), example sidecar yaml for ingestion deployment. Remove TODOs; CI with integration test against etcd + sample flags.

experience.title: What I'd Redesign at Wayfair With 2026 Eyes
experience.subtitle: Ownership · leading two teams · pricing platform
experience.bridge: lensai-dev org is the ownership move — public platform identity, not scattered personal repos.

ai.title: Day 27 — OpenTelemetry Collector as Integration Hub
ai.subtitle: Exporters, processors, and why LensAI speaks OTel natively
ai.hook: Collector processors are middleware — you've shipped worse middleware under more load.

days.thread: smoke.sh exiting 0 is the integration test Blog A's platform narrative and Blog B's OTel lesson both require.

days.code: lensai-integration Day 1 — Create lensai-dev GitHub org; transfer infra-ai-streaming, ebpf-llm-tracer, distributed-flagd under org. Unified docker-compose at lensai-integration/quickstart: all three services + Grafana pre-provisioned. Smoke test script scripts/smoke.sh exits 0 when events flow eBPF → ingest → ClickHouse.

experience.title: Integration Tests — The Only Launch Criteria I Trust
experience.subtitle: Agoda · staging discipline · compose on clean laptop
experience.bridge: Landing page demo GIF must show eBPF → ingest → flagd → Grafana or it's marketing, not engineering.

ai.title: Day 28 — Competitor Teardown — LensAI Positioning
ai.subtitle: Datadog LLM Observability vs Helicone vs Langfuse — honest gaps
ai.hook: Name what you don't do yet — credibility beats feature laundry lists.

days.thread: Product essay draft ties competitor gaps to today's compose quickstart — one narrative, two audiences.

days.code: lensai-integration launch — Product landing page (GitHub Pages): value prop, architecture diagram, demo GIF, install guide, comparison vs Datadog LLM Observability / Helicone / Langfuse (honest gaps). Publish product essay: 'LensAI — why inference observability needs a TSDB brain, not a metrics sidecar.' HN Show HN; OSS-03 OpenTelemetry Collector PR (Kafka or batch processor). Integration test on clean machine.

product_blog.subtitle: Not a metrics sidecar: eBPF + streaming + flags as one product

experience.title: Thirty Days of Building — What Shipped vs What Matter
experience.subtitle: LensAI month-one · public proof

ai.title: Day 29 — The AI Infrastructure Stack — Full Map
ai.subtitle: Training → inference → observability → agents — where you fit
ai.hook: Draw the map once; every month-five product snaps onto it.

days.thread: LensAI launch closes Month 1; tomorrow TraceForge starts because agents are multi-step distributed systems nobody traces well.

days.code: agent-trace-collector — DESIGN.md: agent execution graph, span schema (trace_id, span_id, parent_span_id, tool_name, model, tokens, cost_usd, status, latency_ms), tool taxonomy, OTel mapping.

experience.title: Step 7 Failed Silently — And Nobody Had a Span
experience.subtitle: Delivery Hero · async pipelines · visibility
experience.bridge: agent-trace-collector exists because DH taught me the worst outages are the ones between services you already monitor. Today's code in agent-trace-collector implements that lesson.

ai.title: Day 30 — ReAct Loops as Distributed Workflows
ai.subtitle: Planner → tools → memory — state machine, not magic
ai.hook: Draw the loop as a saga; each tool call is a compensatable step with timeout.

days.thread: Step 7 Failed Silently meets ReAct Loops as Distributed Workflows in today's agent-trace-collector commit.

days.code: OTel Collector pipeline: receivers otlp + kafka; processors batch + attributes; exporters to ClickHouse agent_spans and Kafka agent.spans.v1. Compose overlay on LensAI quickstart.

project_blog.title: Week 5 — agent-trace-collector: Tracing the Silent Step 7
project_blog.subtitle: OTel spans, ClickHouse waterfalls, and the demo agent

experience.title: Tool Calls Are RPCs With Marketing
experience.subtitle: Agoda · fan-out · retries
experience.bridge: Wrapping OpenAI today is no different from instrumenting an internal gRPC service — same span boundaries, different JSON. Today's code in agent-trace-collector implements that lesson.

ai.title: Day 31 — OpenTelemetry Semantics for Agents
ai.subtitle: span kinds, attributes, and what to standardize now
ai.hook: Align attribute names with your ClickHouse columns before v1 ships.

days.thread: Tool Calls Are RPCs With Marketing meets OpenTelemetry Semantics for Agents in today's agent-trace-collector commit.

days.code: Python SDK traceforge.wrap_openai(): emit spans per tool_call, hash arguments, record latency/tokens. Mock server test with parallel tool calls.

experience.title: When the Collector Is the Product

days.code: Embedding worker: call embedding API (OpenAI text-embedding-3-small or local), batch size 32, write vectors to pgvector table prompts( tenant_id, prompt_hash, embedding, created_at ). Idempotent on prompt_hash.

days.code: Cache lookup path: cosine similarity search with threshold 0.92 default, tenant override in config. On hit: return cached completion + emit LensAI event cache_hit=true. On miss: pass through to router.

experience.title: False Positives Have a Dollar Cost
experience.subtitle: Delivery Hero · wrong ETA
experience.bridge: A cache false positive is a wrong ETA — threshold is an SLO. Today's semantic-cache-engine work makes that concrete.

days.code: Cache analytics: hit rate, false positive proxy (user thumbs-down webhook stub), cost saved estimate. Grafana panel + BENCHMARKS.md sweep thresholds 0.88–0.96 on held-out prompt set.
